mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth that drives corporate revenue expectations higher, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to generate positive 60-day returns in over 60% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that force equity repricing downward within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during periods of industrial production contraction, invalidating the supply bottleneck pricing mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicates real rate tightening that compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite concurrent CPI acceleration and rate hikes, or the interaction effect shows negative predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate profit forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns exhibit no positive correlation with payroll growth, or the signal generates negative alpha in more than half of the tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction below zero percent triggers immediate risk-off positioning as investors anticipate earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in over 50% of quarters where GDPC1 registers negative growth, breaking the recession risk-off channel.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation signals healthy capacity utilization that supports corporate margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The positive signal fails to predict 20-day returns when inflation is low, or the mechanism shows negative information coefficient during early-cycle phases.
